NWS STORM SURVEY DETERMINED A SHORT-LIVED EF1 TORNADO (PATH LENGTH APPROXIMATELY 0.11 MILES AND PATH WIDTH APPROXIMATELY 100 YARDS) DESTROYED A SHED AND SNAPPED NUMEROUS TREES. THE TORNADO THREW A TRAILER SOUTHWARD AROUND 40 YARDS WHILE OTHER DEBRIS WAS THROWN TOWARD THE NORTHEAST.

DAMAGE TO THE CITY OF RICHMOND POLICE DEPARTMENT BUILDING RESULTING IN THE LOSS OF SEVERAL OF THE SLATE TILES, ASPHALT SHINGLES, AND GUTTERS. SOME OF THE TILES WERE FOUND 100 YARDS AWAY. IN THE ADJACENT PARK SEVERAL TREES LOST LARGE LIMBS OR WERE KNOCKED OVER INCLUDING A CEDAR TREE WITH THE TOP SNAPPED OFF. ALL DEBRIS APPEARED TO BE DISTRIBUTED IN AN EASTERLY DIRECTION.
